content:
Investigation Summary – Case No. FIR/ACB/BPL/2023/087

The investigation confirms that Mr. Rajiv Sharma, Senior Revenue Officer, Bhopal Municipal Corporation, has acquired assets disproportionate to his known income. The ₹47.6 lakh in unexplained bank inflows, the ₹2.1 crore property purchase under his wife’s name, and the encrypted WhatsApp communications with Vikram Singh all point to systematic bribery.

Although Ms. Priya Mehta’s testimony regarding the March 15, 2022 cash transaction is factually impossible due to verified CCTV and attendance logs, her broader allegations of cash bribes are corroborated by digital forensics and financial records.

The fabricated inheritance document was a deliberate attempt to mislead the investigation. The motive appears to be financing his daughter’s overseas medical treatment, which was denied insurance coverage due to a pre-existing condition. This motive, while sympathetic, does not absolve criminal conduct.

Vikram Singh is identified as the intermediary who facilitated the bribes and acted as the money conduit. He is currently under further investigation.

Recommendation: Charge sheet to be filed against Rajiv Sharma under Section 13(1)(e) of the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988, and Section 420 IPC for cheating. Vikram Singh to be summoned as an accomplice. Priya Mehta’s credibility is compromised on the specific date of March 15, 2022, but her overall testimony remains partially credible.
